personality_one_liner: A high-performance command center built for speed, tracking, and operational focus without visual fluff.

spacing.rule: Dense and utilitarian. Use dividers and tonal backgrounds to separate information, not massive whitespace. Padding should be tight but touch targets must remain 44pt+.

dont's: No blur or glassmorphism. Use solid surfaceSecondary or border lines to establish hierarchy, keeping it feeling like a fast mechanical tool.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: High-level command center showing today's intention, schedule timeline, priority tasks, and habit checklist.

states.loading: Centered minimalist spinner in brand color.
states.empty: Should never be completely empty. Show prompt to set daily intention.

screens.layout: Vertical scroll. Top: Large 'Daily Focus' text input area. Below it: 3-column metric row or single horizontal scroll of 'Upcoming Deliveries'. Next section: 'Today's Schedule' vertical timeline (compact). Next section: 'Today's Habits' list of checkboxes. Bottom: 'Priority Tasks' (overdue items flagged with error text color).

screens.name: Tasks
screens.purpose: A ruthless, fast task manager with a sticky quick-add bar.

states.empty: Minimal architectural image with 'No active tasks. Add one below.'

screens.layout: Top: Segmented controls for Views (Today, This Week, All). List: dense rows (title, due date badge, priority icon). Swipe-right to complete, swipe-left to delete. Bottom: Sticky quick-add text input bar anchored above the tab bar.

screens.name: Schedule (Clients & Projects)
screens.purpose: Combined view for calendar schedule and client projects.

states.empty: Empty state illustration with CTA to add client.

screens.layout: Two tabs at top (Schedule | Clients). Schedule view: Vertical continuous timeline stream of the week. Clients view: Expandable accordion cards per client showing linked projects and deliveries.

screens.name: Habits
screens.purpose: A GitHub-style heatmap grid to track daily fixed habits neutrally.
screens.layout: Top: Current streak counter and weekly completion rate. Main: Matrix grid. Rows are habit names, columns are the last 7 to 14 days (squares). Today's column is highlighted. Tapping a square toggles it. Completed = brandPrimary fill, Missed/Empty = surfaceTertiary fill (NEUTRAL, no red). Bottom: List to manage the 5-7 fixed habits.

dashboard_hero_bg.alt: Dark minimalist architectural texture for dashboard top banner

empty_state_tasks.alt: Minimalist black and white architectural shadow play for empty state

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the Dark-First Utility guidelines. No light mode toggle; the app is permanently in Dark Mode as requested.
For the Habit Tracker, create a GitHub-style heatmap using square grid cells. Empty cells MUST be neutral (surfaceTertiary) with no red/punishment colors. Checked cells use brandPrimary.
Tasks MUST flag overdue items in the 'error' color (#FF3D00).
Ensure the task quick-add bar is always sticky at the bottom, directly above the navigation bar, allowing task addition in under 10 seconds.
Use 'Barlow Condensed' for numbers, metrics, and section headers to give it that command-center/dashboard feel, while 'IBM Plex Sans' should be used for readable task titles.
Implement zero-glassmorphism. Use strict borders (border, borderStrong) and solid backgrounds (surfaceSecondary) to define containers.
Include a 'Daily Focus' single-line text field prominently at the top of the Dashboard.
Ensure swipe gestures (react-native-gesture-handler) are used for quick task completion.
Haptics: Provide sharp tactile feedback on task completion and habit check-offs, do not over-use on scroll.
